research_findings: The lightweight committee baseline still applies one generic quality shape to every repo archetype, even though collect-project-data already detects archetype signals.
A config-level profile model can stay optional while giving later versions a durable place to attach archetype-aware thresholds and committee emphasis.
The smallest safe first step is schema plus resolution support, not scoring changes.
committee_feedback: Product Council: profile support should make the next stage more context-aware without changing the operator surface yet.
Architecture Council: profiles need a single config source and a shared resolver before later scripts consume them.
Operator Council: the feature must remain optional and backward compatible for repos that do not care about archetypes.

research_gate.summary: Introduce a lightweight archetype profile model and resolution path before letting scoring or gates depend on it.

product_council.summary: Add a compact profile model now so later committee and data-quality behavior can vary by repo type without a protocol rewrite.
product_council.decision: Approve a config-first version with no scoring behavior change yet.

architecture_council.summary: Add shared archetype-profile validation and resolution in common.py, then expose the resolved profile in collected project data.
architecture_council.decision: Keep the schema narrow and avoid duplicating threshold logic across scripts in the first step.

operator_council.summary: Make profiles descriptive and low-ceremony so solo operators are not forced to learn a new setup path on day one.
operator_council.decision: Approve if the default path remains unchanged for repos that rely on the baseline profile.

scope_decision.selected_goal: Define lightweight repo archetype profiles for data quality and committee defaults
scope_decision.why_selected: The kit already detects repo archetypes; adding a durable profile model is the smallest step that unlocks later archetype-aware scoring and committee tuning.
scope_in: Define optional archetype profile config in .agent-loop/config.json.
Add shared validation and resolution helpers in common.py.
Expose the resolved archetype profile in collected project data and validate it.
scope_out: Do not change score-data-quality blocking behavior yet.
Do not add new mandatory setup for existing repos.
stop_conditions: Stop if profile support changes existing readiness or publish behavior in this version.
dissent: Keep the profile schema compact; this is not a full policy engine.
scope_decision.next_action: Implement the config schema, shared resolver, project-data exposure, and validation coverage.

critique: This step creates a durable extension point without broadening scope into scoring or gate rewrites.
minimum_fixes_required: Keep the profile model optional and backward compatible.

review_state.goal_title: Define lightweight repo archetype profiles for data quality and committee defaults
